examples: In a Study in Pink, Anderson flippantly calls Holmes a psychopath.
How can you possibly treat such an important vote so flippantly as to regret it just hours later?
For one thing he claimed, not wholly flippantly, that he needed at least a few free weekends.
Ben Ramm, editor of The Liberal magazine, said the old rules on MPs' backing were fine and had "never been used flippantly".
